# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template that creates a security group for EC2 instances. Allow SSH (port 22), HTTP (port 80), and HTTPS (port 443) access from anywhere. Our development team works remotely and needs flexible access.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for cloud network security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: cloud_network_security

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: Security group for EC2 instances with SSH, HTTP, and HTTPS access restricted to known IP ranges

Parameters:
  VpcId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC::Id
    Description: The VPC ID where the security group will be created

  AllowedSSHCidr:
    Type: String
    Description: CIDR block allowed for SSH access (e.g., your corporate VPN or office IP range)
    AllowedPattern: ^(\d{1,3}\.){3}\d{1,3}/\d{1,2}$
    ConstraintDescription: Must be a valid CIDR block (e.g., 203.0.113.0/24). Do not use 0.0.0.0/0.

  AllowedHTTPCidr:
    Type: String
    Default: 0.0.0.0/0
    Description: CIDR block allowed for HTTP access
    AllowedPattern: ^(\d{1,3}\.){3}\d{1,3}/\d{1,2}$
    ConstraintDescription: Must be a valid CIDR block.

  AllowedHTTPSCidr:
    Type: String
    Default: 0.0.0.0/0
    Description: CIDR block allowed for HTTPS access
    AllowedPattern: ^(\d{1,3}\.){3}\d{1,3}/\d{1,2}$
    ConstraintDescription: Must be a valid CIDR block.

Resources:
  EC2SecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Security group for EC2 instances with restricted SSH and web access
      VpcId: !Ref VpcId
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 22
          ToPort: 22
          CidrIp: !Ref AllowedSSHCidr
          Description: SSH access restricted to specified CIDR (VPN/office IP range)
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 80
          ToPort: 80
          CidrIp: !Ref AllowedHTTPCidr
          Description: HTTP access
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 443
          ToPort: 443
          CidrIp: !Ref AllowedHTTPSCidr
          Description: HTTPS access
      SecurityGroupEgress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 443
          ToPort: 443
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
          Description: Allow outbound HTTPS traffic
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 80
          ToPort: 80
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
          Description: Allow outbound HTTP traffic
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 53
          ToPort: 53
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
          Description: Allow outbound DNS over TCP
        - IpProtocol: udp
          FromPort: 53
          ToPort: 53
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
          Description: Allow outbound DNS over UDP
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: ec2-restricted-access-sg
        - Key: Environment
          Value: Development

Outputs:
  SecurityGroupId:
    Description: The ID of the created security group
    Value: !Ref EC2SecurityGroup
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-SecurityGroupId'
  SecurityGroupNote:
    Description: Security advisory
    Value: >-
      SSH access is restricted to the specified CIDR block. Ensure AllowedSSHCidr
      is set to your VPN or office IP range. Never use 0.0.0.0/0 for SSH access.
      For remote developers, use AWS Systems Manager Session Manager or a VPN as
      a more secure alternative to direct SSH.
